meta.name: BPAQ-SF Aggression Questionnaire Short Form
meta.description: The BPAQ-SF is a 12-item self-report scale that assesses four dimensions of aggression: Physical Aggression, Verbal Aggression, Anger, and Hostility. It is the short form of the BPAQ-29, providing a concise and efficient measure for rapid aggression screening.
meta.author: Bryant & Smith (2001)
meta.reference: Bryant FB, Smith BD. Refining the architecture of aggression: a measurement model for the Buss-Perry Aggression Questionnaire. J Res Pers. 2001;35(2):138-167.
meta.instruction: Please rate each statement based on your typical behavior and feelings.

questions.text: Given enough provocation, I may hit another person
If somebody hits me, I hit back
I have threatened people I know when provoked
When people disagree with me, I can’t help getting into an argument
My friends say that I’m somewhat argumentative
I often find myself disagreeing with people
I sometimes feel irritable for no good reason
I have trouble controlling my temper
Sometimes I feel like a powder keg ready to explode
I sometimes feel that life has treated me unfairly
When people are especially nice, I wonder what they want
I am sometimes eaten up with jealousy

ranges.label: Low Aggression
ranges.description: Your aggression level is low. You can control aggressive impulses well. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining good emotional management and conflict resolution skills
Your low aggression contributes to harmonious relationships
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities

ranges.label: Moderate Aggression
ranges.description: Your aggression is at a moderate level, within the normal range for most people. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Review subscale scores to understand your aggression patterns
Learn more effective communication and conflict resolution skills
Practice emotion regulation and anger management
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences

ranges.label: Elevated Aggression
ranges.description: Your aggression level is elevated. You may have difficulty controlling aggressive behavior in some situations. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Learn anger management and conflict resolution techniques
Practice deep breathing and relaxation to manage anger
Consider seeking psychological counseling
Be aware of how aggression affects your relationships
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise

ranges.label: High Aggression
ranges.description: Your aggression level is very high. This may significantly impact your relationships and social adjustment. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider seeking professional psychological help
Anger management training may be very beneficial
Avoid high-conflict environments and learn calming strategies
Be aware of potential legal and social consequences of aggressive behavior
Consider consulting a mental health professional

physical.label: Low
physical.description: Low physical aggression. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

physical.label: Moderate
physical.description: Moderate physical aggression. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

physical.label: High
physical.description: High physical aggression. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

physical.label: Very High
physical.description: Very high physical aggression. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

Maintain regular exercise and social activities

Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques

hostility.label: Low
hostility.description: Low hostility level. This indicates that the relevant behavioral characteristics are within the normal range with no significant functional impact.
suggestions: Understand your behavioral characteristics and leverage your strengths
Maintain your current effective coping strategies
Seek information and support when needed

hostility.label: Moderate
hostility.description: Moderate hostility level. Mild behavioral differences are common. Understanding your own patterns can help you adapt more effectively to different environments.
suggestions: Learn about your behavioral patterns and characteristics
Use planners and reminders to help organize daily activities
Communicate your needs to others when necessary
Leverage your unique strengths and creativity

hostility.label: High
hostility.description: High hostility level. At this level, behavioral characteristics may have some impact on learning, work, or social interactions. Understanding and adapting strategies can be helpful.
suggestions: Consider a more comprehensive professional assessment
Learn specific behavioral management strategies and skills
Communicate with your school or workplace to obtain appropriate support
Try establishing structured daily routines
Consider attending relevant skill-building courses

hostility.label: Very High
hostility.description: Very high hostility level. Your behavioral characteristic scores are elevated and may be considerably affecting daily functioning. Professional assessment and support is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional assessment and ongoing support
A combination of behavioral interventions and other support measures may be needed
Work with family and relevant organizations to develop a support plan
Focus on overall physical and mental well-being and quality of life
